title: The Envelope
caption: Forty-seven pages. Twelve photographs. Three letters. And one love note from 1963, written in careful cursive by a woman who believed this corner of the earth was worth keeping. Harriet pressed the seal onto the envelope with the steadiness of someone who had been waiting sixty years to do exactly this. Chloe carried it up the steps of the City Planning Office herself, Mr. Chen beside her, the two of them quiet in the way of people who understand the weight of something. And then it was done. Back at Marco's, the four of them sat together at the corner table as evening came on — Marco with his cake, Harriet with her folded hands, Mr. Chen with his patient silence, and Chloe with her coffee and a small, quiet smile. The submission was in. Now they waited. And waiting, for once, felt almost like peace.
location: Marco's Bakery; City Planning Office steps
story_notes: The heritage submission is completed and delivered to the City Planning Office. The community now enters a waiting period to learn if the garden's historical designation will be confirmed.
